instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What is a 'zero' cost term plan? Who should buy it?
A term life insurance policy is usually a pure protection plan. It covers the risk of premature death during the coverage duration. If you survive the plan tenure, you usually don’t get any maturity benefit. Moreover, if you discontinue premium payments during the policy tenure, you lose out on the premium paid as well as the coverage benefits.
The loss of the premium and coverage benefits is an unattractive feature that many policyholders dislike. That is why a new concept of term life insurance plans has been launched called zero-cost term plans. Let’s understand what they are all about.
What is a zero-cost term plan1?
A zero cost term insurance plan is a new innovation in term plans wherein you get a benefit even if you discontinue premium payment during the policy tenure. Under these plans, the premiums are refunded if you surrender the policy before the chosen term.
The idea behind these zero cost term insurance plans is that you might not need term insurance coverage at later stages of your life when all your financial goals are fulfilled. In such cases, the plan gives you the liberty to terminate the coverage and get the premium refunded back.
Existing categories of term insurance
Excluding zero-cost term plans, there are different types of term plans that are available in the market. These include the following –
Zero cost term plan v/s return of premium term plan
Like the zero-cost policy, a return of premium term plan (TROP) also refunds the premiums if you survive the policy tenure. However, both these plans are different from one another on one main aspect – the refund condition.
Under a term plan with return of premium, the refund happens when the policy matures and you are alive. You cannot claim the refund during the policy tenure. If you surrender the policy during the tenure, you might get a surrender value, but it would be lower than the premiums that you have paid. However, under zero-cost plans, you get a refund even during the policy tenure.
Zero-cost plans are a new concept in the term insurance market. So far, only a few insurers have launched this type of plan. So, if you are looking for these plans, you need to check for the insurers that offer them and also understand the other benefits of the policy.
That being said, remember that a term insurance plan is taken for financial security. If you are looking for plans that you want to surrender, then zero-cost plans would be effective. However, once you surrender, you lose coverage and financial security. So, you can explore comprehensive term insurance plans that provide the ideal coverage at competitive premiums.
Bajaj Allianz Life offers different types of term plans which offer all-round protection. You can choose from whole life coverage to return premium options. There are plans that offer inbuilt and optional riders so that you get complete protection against any emergency that life might throw your way.
Conclusion
It is recommended that you buy a term plan with a long-term horizon and don’t think about surrendering it midway. This would help you get complete financial security throughout your life. While zero-cost plans allow a surrender benefit, their premiums would be higher compared to a regular-term insurance plan. If your goals are limited and will be fulfilled soon, opt for a plan with a lower coverage duration rather than surrendering it midway. Such plans would allow the intended coverage at lower rates of premiums without you having to surrender. Moreover, the premiums that you save on such plans can be invested in different avenues to earn a return on them.
So, assess your needs and then invest in a suitable term insurance policy, whether it is a zero-cost
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
